channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Filing in Trump case details remarkable schism with Pence over rejecting 2020 election loss
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Days before rioters roamed the halls of the U.S. Capitol threatening to “hang Mike Pence,” Donald Trump told his vice president that people are going to “hate your guts” and “think you're stupid” if he failed to stop the 2020 election certification.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Assassination attempts and new threats have reshaped how Donald Trump campaigns
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump was onstage at a rally on Long Island last month, talking about taxes, when he appeared momentarily spooked by something he'd spotted over his shoulder. “I thought this was a wise guy coming up,” he explained, joking that he was getting his elbow ready to fight back.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to headline NRA event in pivotal swing state two weeks before Election Day
item.description: Former President Trump will headline the NRA's Defend the 2nd event in Savannah, Georgia, Oct. 22, rallying support for the Second Amendment two weeks before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colin Allred narrows gap in Texas Senate race, but can he win?
item.description: The Democratic congressman is polling closer to Sen. Ted Cruz in his challenge this year, and independent race rankers are noticing.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Matthew Choi, KPRC 2

item.title: Assemblyman Eddie Gibbs arrested during traffic stop: NYPD
item.description: EAST HARLEM, Manhattan (PIX11) – New York state Assemblyman Eddie Gibbs was arrested during a traffic stop in Manhattan on Thursday, according to the NYPD. The East Harlem Democrat was taken into custody in front of 1844 Lexington Ave. around 1:30 p.m., police said.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Connecticut absentee voting begins
item.description: Connecticut joins a growing majority of states that have begun absentee or early voting as the 2024 election gets underway. Connecticut began absentee voting Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Californians' crime concerns put pressure on criminal justice reform and progressive DAs
item.description: Ten years ago, 3.7 million Californians voted for a landmark measure that made many nonviolent crimes misdemeanors instead of felonies.
item.dc:creator: Jaimie Ding And Trân Nguyễn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Massachusetts governor implements new gun law weeks ahead of schedule
item.description: Massachusetts Gov. Maura Healey was ahead of schedule Wednesday, when she implemented sweeping gun reform expected to go into law later in October.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Melania Trump says she supports abortion rights, putting her at odds with the GOP
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Melania Trump revealed her support for abortion rights Thursday ahead of the release of her upcoming memoir, exposing a stark contrast with her husband, Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump, on the crucial election issue.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics over Humanity? How Hurricane Helene impacts the political field
item.description: Politics over Humanity? How Hurricane Helene impacts the Political field as the race to the White House is weeks away.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump and the federal election case against him: Key passages from prosecutors' latest court filing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump “laid the groundwork for his crimes” well before Election Day in 2020. He said “the details don’t matter” when told his election fraud claims would fail in court.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's campaign says he raised $160 million in September, ended month with $283 million banked
item.description: Former President Donald Trump raised $160 million for his campaign in September and entered October with $283 million in the bank for the campaign's final sprint, his aides announced.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Over 1.7M migrants who could pose national security risk arrived in US during Biden admin: report
item.description: More than 1.7 million migrants who are identified as "special interest aliens" have been encountered at the border under the Biden administration, according to a House report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Politics Friday: Minnesota’s 2nd Congressional District Debate
item.description: Minnesota’s 2nd Congressional District is one of the most closely watched races in the nation. Coming up Friday at noon, MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst moderates a debate between Democratic Rep. Angie Craig and Republican candidate Joe Teirab.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Ohio GOP Senate candidate Moreno hits Brown on Inflation Reduction Act vote in ad that's part of $25M buy
item.description: Ohio Republican Bernie Moreno slammed Sen. Sherrod Brown for supporting the Inflation Reduction Act in a new ad shown to Fox News Digital in advance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man convicted for dozens of threats against former Speaker McCarthy over Chinese balloons
item.description: A man from Montana was convicted by a federal jury this week on the charges of threatening former House Speaker Kevin McCarthy over the course of more than 100 phone calls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Melania Trump: ‘We are dealing with much more danger’
item.description: Former first lady Melania Trump weighed in on the state of former President Donald Trump's bid for the White House in an interview Wednesday, while also reflecting on the recent assassination attempts on her husband's life.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump on Springfield Haitian migrants: 'They have to be removed'
item.description: The former president spoke to NewsNation's Ali Bradley about his plans regarding the Haitian migrants living in Springfield, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Watch: FOX59/CBS4 Indiana Governor Debate
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS -- For the first time this election year, the two major party candidates are having the opportunity to participate in a televised debate surrounding issues that are important for Indiana voters. U.S. Senator Mike Braun, R-Ind.
item.dc:creator: David Gay, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats see signs of growing momentum in Texas Senate race
item.description: Democrats are seeing signs of growing momentum in the Texas Senate race between Sen. Ted Cruz (R) and Rep. Colin Allred (D), fueling the party’s hopes that this year could be the breakthrough they’ve been seeking for multiple cycles.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota abortion law signed by Walz scrutinized during VP debate
item.description: Internet searches about Minnesota’s abortion law — the first state to codify access after the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade — were high during Tuesday night’s vice presidential debate.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: From Walz’s hometown to an American Legion, Minnesotans react to governor in VP debate
item.description: Supporters of the Republican and Democratic presidential tickets gathered Tuesday evening for debate watch parties around Minnesota for the likely only debate between Sen. J.D. Vance of Ohio and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, Matt Sepic, and Nicole Ki, MPR News

item.title: In VP debate, Walz acknowledges he wasn’t in China during Tiananmen uprising
item.description: APM Reports, the investigations and documentaries unit of MPR News, first reported that Walz’s claim during a 2014 congressional hearing on the 25th anniversary of the massacre didn’t square up with media accounts in 1989.
item.dc:creator: Tom Scheck, Curtis Gilbert, Caspar von Au, and Forest Hunt, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he would veto national abortion ban
item.description: Former President Trump on Tuesday said he would veto a federal abortion ban if it made it to his desk in a potential second term after previously refusing to say whether he would do so.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump downplays troop injuries in 2020 missile strike: 'You mean because they had a headache?'
item.description: "So first of all, injured. What does injured mean? Injured means — you mean because they had a headache? Because the bombs never hit the fort," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: NY race for US Senate: Sapraicone on the border, abortion, gun control
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – With a Senate seat up for grabs, Republican Mike Sapraicone joined PIX11 News on Tuesday night to discuss gun control, abortion, crime, and other key issues. The retired NYPD detective will take on incumbent Democratic Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand in the Nov. 5 general election.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: Trump leads with 49 percent support, Harris earns 47 percent support, Green Party candidate Jill Stein gets 1 percent support and Justice For All Party candidate Cornel West gets 1 percent support in the Quinnipiac University poll conducted in late September and released Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Volunteers from deep blue Illinois learn to canvas in battleground states
item.description: Across the seven states likely to decide the presidential election, thousands of volunteers, including some from Illinois, are on the ground going door-to-door working to increase voter turnout.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Israel begins ground offensive in Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military pressed its ground incursion into southern Lebanon on Tuesday, calling the operations “limited incursions” that are targeting Hezbollah militants.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin, Kat Lonsdorf, and Miguel Macias, MPR News

item.title: How young Republicans and Democrats are whipping up votes in key districts
item.description: As the presidential race ramps up in Georgia, one vital voting demographic is mobilizing and hoping to impact the race: young people.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly, Erika Ryan, Kira Wakeam, and Courtney Dorning, MPR News

item.title: As debate rages over coverage, rural Minnesotans face challenges getting fertility care
item.description: For many rural Minnesotans getting treatments is still really hard and expensive. The stark reality is 1 in 6 in the state are currently experiencing infertility, and for those living outside the metro, treatment options are especially limited.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Trump suggests ‘1 rough hour’ of policing would end retail crime
item.description: The former president called for law enforcement to be freed up to "do their job" without restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump suspect pleads not guilty in attempted assassination
item.description: The man accused of attempting to assassinate former President Trump at one of his Florida golf courses earlier this month has pleaded not guilty to five federal charges, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers fear 'chaos' if Trump-Harris race goes to the wire
item.description: Lawmakers in both parties are bracing themselves for a messy aftermath to Election Day as polls show the race between former President Trump and Vice President Harris is so close in several battleground states that it could take days to determine the winner.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Exploring the Eric Adams indictment for bribery, wire fraud, and solicitating contributions from Turkish nationals
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams pleaded not guilty in a federal bribery case after being indicted on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump tops Harris by 6 points in Arizona: Survey
item.description: The new USA TODAY/Suffolk University survey found Trump leads the vice president, 48 percent to 42 percent, in the Grand Canyon State.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5
